title: Robust race-length estimation
summary: Improve derived race-progress features by estimating total laps carefully and flagging unreliable progress calculations.
rationale: Many successful feature blocks depend on estimated laps remaining. If that base estimate is unstable, downstream finish-window and stop-window features inherit noise.
implementation_hint: Derive estimated total laps as `LapNumber / RaceProgress` with guards for zero or missing progress, clip to a plausible range, add estimated laps remaining, progress-squared, early/mid/late/final-phase flags, and invalid-progress indicators.
expected_effect: Medium stability benefit with medium-high confidence; direct gain may be small but downstream features become safer.
risk: Overly aggressive clipping could hide short-race or synthetic edge cases; expose both clipped and missingness flags.
